title: 11 Herbal Creams For Burping
intro_desc: Herbal creams for burping may not be as well-known as teas, but they can be just as effective. Burping, also known as belching, is a natural way for your body to release gas from your digestive system. However, excessive burping can be uncomfortable and embarrassing. That's where herbal teas come in – they can help relieve burping by soothing your digestive system and reducing inflammation in your stomach.  Herbal teas work by using the natural properties of herbs to calm your digestive system. For example, Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce stomach pain and discomfort. Foeniculum vulgare, or fennel, has a calming effect on the digestive system and can help reduce gas and bloating. Cinnamomum verum, or cinnamon, has antibacterial properties that can help prevent the growth of bad bacteria in your stomach, which can contribute to burping.  Using herbal teas to relieve burping can bring many benefits to your life. For one, it can help you feel more comfortable and confident. When you're not worrying about embarrassing burps, you can focus on other things and enjoy your meals without discomfort. Additionally, using herbal teas can help you develop healthier eating habits. By soothing your digestive system, herbal teas can help you digest your food more efficiently, which can lead to a reduced risk of digestive problems and other health issues.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active constituents like gingerols and shogaols, which are known for their anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to relax the muscles in the digestive system, reducing the frequency and severity of burping. The oils present in the cream, such as ginger oil, have a calming effect on the digestive tract, allowing food to pass through more smoothly. The warming sensation caused by ginger's thermogenic properties can also help to increase digestion and reduce bloating. As a result, Zingiber officinale creams can provide relief from excessive burping and promote a healthier digestive system.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for burping
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of dried Zingiber officinale root in a double boiler.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in the double boiler over low heat, stirring until smooth.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried Zingiber officinale root to the melted mixture and stir for 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool for 10 minutes. Then, strain it through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Pour the cooled mixture into a container and store it in the fridge. Apply a small amount to the chest or abdomen as needed to help alleviate burping.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare creams contains essential oils rich in active constituents like fenchone, anethole, and estragole. These compounds are known for their carminative properties, which help to relieve gas and bloating in the digestive system. Fenchone, in particular, has been shown to have a direct effect on the nervous system, slowing down the movement of gas through the digestive tract and reducing the likelihood of burping. Anethole, on the other hand, has antispasmodic properties, which help to calm down the muscles in the digestive tract and prevent spasms that can lead to burping. By reducing gas and muscle spasms, Foeniculum vulgare creams can help to alleviate the discomfort associated with burping.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal creams for burping
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Foeniculum vulgare seeds, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of shea butter.
Heat the coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat. Add the dried Foeniculum vulgare seeds and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool for a few minutes. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, discarding the seeds.
Add the shea butter to the bowl and mix well until it's fully incorporated. Let the mixture cool and thicken at room temperature.
Once the mixture has thickened, transfer it to an airtight container and store it in the fridge for up to 2 weeks. Apply a small amount to the belly as needed to help soothe burping.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum creams contains a compound called cineole, which has been found to have carminative properties. These properties help to reduce gas and alleviate symptoms of burping. Cinnamomum verum creams also contain eugenol, which is known for its ability to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing gas to be released more easily. The anti-inflammatory properties of eugenol can also help to soothe the stomach and reduce irritation, further alleviating symptoms of burping. Additionally, Cinnamomum verum creams contain linalool, which can help to calm the digestive system and promote a sense of relaxation, further reducing the occurrence of burping.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal creams for burping
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 10 drops of Cinnamomum verum essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler. Heat until both are completely liquid.
Add beeswax to the melted mixture. Stir until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool for 5 minutes. Add Cinnamomum verum essential oil and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container. Let it cool and solidify completely before using. Store in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains a compound called curcumin, which is a powerful antioxidant. This antioxidant has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the digestive system and reduce irritation in the esophagus and stomach. Curcuma longa creams also contain turmeric's active constituents, including demethoxycurcumin, bisdemethoxycurcumin, and turmerone, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects. These properties can help alleviate symptoms of burping, such as heartburn and acid reflux, by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and promoting the healing of the esophagus. The curcumin in Curcuma longa creams has been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body, which can contribute to burping and digestive discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for burping
remedy_procedure: Mix 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder (Curcuma longa) with 1 cup of coconut oil in a bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each heating, until the oil is warm and the turmeric is dissolved.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl to remove any lumps or sediment.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the bowl and melt in a double boiler or microwave.
Pour the mixture into a jar or container and let it cool and solidify. Store the Curcuma longa cream in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cuminum cyminum creams contains several bioactive constituents, including cuminaldehyde, linalool, and borneol. These compounds have carminative properties, which help to relieve gas and reduce the occurrence of burping. Cuminaldehyde, in particular, has been shown to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing gas to pass through more easily. Linalool and borneol have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe the digestive system and reduce irritation that may contribute to burping. By combining these properties, Cuminum cyminum creams may be helpful in reducing the frequency and severity of burping.
remedies.image_alt: Cuminum cyminum herbal creams for burping
remedy_procedure: Collect 250ml of coconut oil, 100g of cumin seeds, and a small pot for heating.
Heat the coconut oil in the pot over low heat, then add the cumin seeds.
Let the mixture simmer for 10 minutes, then turn off the heat and let it cool.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, and discard the solids.
Store the cumin cream in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains licorice extract, which has active constituents like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids. Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the digestive tract, reducing irritation and inflammation that can cause burping. Flavonoids have antioxidant properties that protect the digestive system from damage and promote healing of the mucous membranes. Glycyrrhizin also has a soothing effect on the stomach muscles, relaxing them and reducing spasms that can lead to burping. By using these creams, the digestive system is given a chance to heal and function properly, reducing the occurrence of burping.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for burping
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Glycerin, and 2 teaspoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until combined.
Add Glycerin and Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder to the mixture and stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, use a spoon to scoop out a small amount and apply to the affected area as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Coriandrum sativum creams contains a compound called linalool, which is a natural oil that helps to calm digestive issues. The creams also contain limonene and beta-pinene, which are terpenes that have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help to soothe the stomach and reduce irritation that can cause burping. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Coriandrum sativum creams, such as kaempferol and quercetin, also have anti-inflammatory effects that can help to calm digestive issues. By applying Coriandrum sativum creams to the stomach, the active constituents can help to reduce inflammation and irritation, making it a potential solution for people who experience persistent burping.
remedies.image_alt: Coriandrum sativum herbal creams for burping
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Coriandrum sativum seeds and 1 cup of coconut oil.
Soak the Coriandrum sativum seeds in 4 cups of water for 2 hours.
Strain the Coriandrum sativum seeds and mix with the coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, then let it cool.
Strain the mixture again and store it in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum creams contains the active constituents piperine, piperonal, and limonene. These compounds have carminative properties, which help to relieve gas and reduce burping. Piperine, in particular, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help to soothe the digestive system and reduce discomfort caused by burping. The antispasmodic properties of piperonal and limonene also help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to burping. By using piper nigrum creams, you may be able to reduce the frequency and severity of burping.
remedies.image_alt: Piper nigrum herbal creams for burping
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of Piper nigrum seeds, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments.
Add the Piper nigrum seeds to the melted mixture and stir well.
Remove from heat and let it cool until it reaches 120°F to 130°F.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams contains menthol and menthone, two active constituents that help relieve burping. Menthol has a cooling effect on the stomach, which can ease digestive discomfort. Menthone has a relaxing effect on the smooth muscle in the stomach, reducing spasms and contractions that can cause burping. The antispasmodic properties of menthone also help to calm the digestive system, reducing irritation and inflammation. By soothing the stomach and calming the digestive system, Mentha x piperita creams can help alleviate the symptoms of burping.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for burping
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of dried Mentha x piperita leaves.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of boiling water.
Steep the dried Mentha x piperita leaves in the distilled water for 10-15 minutes, then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Combine the strained Mentha x piperita water with the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture, stirring until well combined.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers or jars, and let cool and solidify before use as a cream for burping.

remedies.remedy_desc: Anethum graveolens creams contains active constituents like dill apiole and dill ether, which are known for their carminative properties. These properties help to relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce gas production, making it easier to manage burping. Dill apiole also has a direct effect on the digestive system, where it helps to slow down the movement of food through the digestive tract, allowing for better digestion and less gas buildup. Additionally, the anethol in Anethum graveolens creams has a soothing effect on the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to burping. By using Anethum graveolens creams, people can experience relief from burping and other digestive issues due to its ability to regulate digestion and reduce stomach discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Anethum graveolens herbal creams for burping
remedy_procedure: Get 1 cup of dried Anethum graveolens (dill weed) and 2 cups of water in a saucepan. Heat the water until it boils.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. Strain the mixture and let it cool.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a separate saucepan. Heat it until the beeswax melts.
Combine the cooled Anethum graveolens mixture and melted beeswax mixture. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool. Once set, your Anethum graveolens cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Pimpinella anisum creams contains a compound called anethol, which is a key active constituent responsible for its effectiveness in reducing burping. Anethol has a relaxing effect on the smooth muscles in the digestive system, helping to ease spasms and discomfort that can lead to burping. Another active constituent, estragole, also contributes to the calming effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and irritation that can cause gas and bloating. The combination of anethol and estragole in Pimpinella anisum creams helps to soothe the digestive system, making it easier to digest food and reducing the likelihood of burping. By relaxing the muscles and reducing inflammation, Pimpinella anisum creams can help to alleviate the symptoms of burping and provide relief.
remedies.image_alt: Pimpinella anisum herbal creams for burping
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of water, 1 cup of sugar, 1/4 cup of Pimpinella anisum seeds, and a medium saucepan.
Combine water and sugar in the saucepan and heat on medium heat, stirring until sugar dissolves.
Add Pimpinella anisum seeds to the saucepan and reduce heat to low. Let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl. Discard the seeds.
Let the mixture cool and then refrigerate it. Once chilled, apply a small amount to your chest or abdomen to help with burping.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help reduce burping. Ginger tea, in particular, has natural properties that can calm the digestive system. As the body processes the tea, it may ease the discomfort that leads to burping. Some people find that certain herbal teas help them digest food more smoothly, which could result in less burping.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas daily for burping can be safe, but it depends on the ingredients. Some teas, like peppermint and ginger, are commonly used and considered gentle. However, teas with strong herbs like licorice root or chamomile might interact with medications or worsen underlying conditions. Moderate use and knowing the ingredients is key.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with burping, but how long it takes to show results varies. Some people notice a difference within a few days, while others take a week or two. It usually depends on the type of tea and individual tolerance. Drinking the tea regularly can increase the chances of relief from burping.
faq_time: If you're prone to burping, drinking herbal tea right after meals can help. The stomach is working to digest food around this time, and the tea may ease digestive issues that lead to burping. Try having a cup of herbal tea about an hour after eating to see if it makes a difference in how you feel.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for burping. Some herbs like peppermint and ginger can slow down or speed up the way your body absorbs certain medications. This can change how the medications work, making them less or more effective. Be cautious when drinking herbal teas with medications to avoid unexpected effects.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant, it's best to be careful with herbal teas. Some herbal teas, like pennyroyal and sage, can cause problems. They might slow down your baby's growth or even cause a miscarriage. But other herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, are usually safe in small amounts. Just be sure to check the ingredients.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can help with burping, but it's essential to choose the right ones while breastfeeding. Some teas, like peppermint and chamomile, are generally considered safe in moderation. However, teas like sage and pennyroyal may not be suitable for breastfeeding mothers, as they can potentially affect milk production or baby's health. Always check the ingredients and follow the recommended amounts.
title_2: The Burping Dilemma in the Elderly: 11 Herbal Creams to Help
